name: Kira Reports to Cross
description: Kira has eight months of altitude readings that now, combined with Karee's ground data, show the Grid's heartbeat from above. General Cross is the Aetherian command authority with the standing to act on what the fleet has found.
accept_text: She pulls two folders from her chart station — her altitude log and Karee's timing data, now side by side. 'Eight months of lattice readings from eight thousand meters and eight months of ground-pulse data from Terran front-line position, and they're the same wave.' She puts them in a sealed document case. 'Cross needs to see this. Not as a curiosity — as a strategic assessment.' She looks at the case. 'The Grid is reactivating on a consistent schedule. The nodes come online in sequence. I can project the sequence forward. In the time projection, based on the current rate, I can identify approximately when the next visible coherence peak will occur and where.' She pauses. 'That's actionable intelligence. It changes the assessment of the Wastes. It changes the assessment of the Armistice territory. It changes what the High Council should be planning for.' She meets your eyes. 'I've been sending standard reports up the chain for eight months. Standard reports go into a system that files them. This needs to go to Cross directly.' She hands you the case. 'Tell him it's from Kira. Tell him the ground data is from a Terran soldier who noticed the same thing we're measuring from altitude without instruments or training, and whose readings are consistent with ours to the hour.' She pauses. 'He'll want to know about the Terran source. Tell him the Armistice is real in the places where it matters.'
complete_text: Cross reads without stopping, which is how he reads things he considers serious. He puts down Karee's folder and picks up Kira's log and reads the comparison chart twice. Then he sets everything on the table and looks at the wall with the map of the Wastes. 'The Grid's construction sequence,' he says. 'Observable from above and below simultaneously, correlating to the hour.' He is quiet. 'The nodes that are coming online — the sequence Kira has projected — three of those positions are within current Armistice territory. One of them is within what we'd designated as disputed territorial approach.' He pauses. 'Which means the Grid's reactivation is going to produce observable events within the Armistice zone over the next season, regardless of what either government decides.' He looks at the case. 'The Terran ground observer.' He says it with the quality of a commanding officer evaluating a source. 'Front-line position, eight months of consistent data, no instruments. That's a reliable observer who noticed something significant and wrote it down without being told to.' He looks at the map. 'I need to know if Commander Serel has this data. Not whether Serel knows Karee is recording — whether Serel has reviewed what Karee recorded and made a command decision about it.' He picks up a pen. 'Take this communication to Commander Serel. It's a joint coordination request, Aetherian command to Terran command, regarding observed activity in Armistice-adjacent territory. It names the data specifically.' He hands you the sealed letter. 'I want to know what she says.'
